contrast-pairs.$description: Valid contrast pairs, keyed by the two CSS color properties in alphabetical order joined with '<>'. Only foregrounds that require contrast against background-color; exempt properties (flood/lighting/stop/box-shadow-color) omitted. Derived from the webref color-property set.
contrast-pairs.$tierResolution: The tier (text vs non-text) is NOT fixed by the property — it comes from the NODE carrying the foreground: does it render text, at what size (large → 4.5:1), text-element or shape? The property only proposes a candidate.
contrast-pairs.$treeResolution: Use RESOLVED (computed) colors per node — not the cascade/inheritance chain. A concrete pair is a parent→child edge whose resolved colors DIFFER; if parent and child resolve to the same color there is no pair (nothing to distinguish). The background side is the parent's resolved background-color, the foreground the child's resolved color.

-webkit-text-fill-color<>background-colo.$note: The tier (text 7:1/4.5:1 or non-text 3:1) and the exact ratio come from the NODE carrying `-webkit-text-fill-color`: does it render text, and at what size (large text → 4.5:1)? The property only proposes the candidate.
-webkit-text-fill-color<>background-colo.$check: Use RESOLVED colors. A concrete pair is a parent→child edge where the resolved `-webkit-text-fill-color` differs from the parent's resolved background-color; equal colors are skipped (nothing to distinguish).

-webkit-text-stroke<>background-color.$note: The tier (text 7:1/4.5:1 or non-text 3:1) and the exact ratio come from the NODE carrying `-webkit-text-stroke`: does it render text, and at what size (large text → 4.5:1)? The property only proposes the candidate.
-webkit-text-stroke<>background-color.$check: Use RESOLVED colors. A concrete pair is a parent→child edge where the resolved `-webkit-text-stroke` differs from the parent's resolved background-color; equal colors are skipped (nothing to distinguish).
